Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Kim Knapp
Off Calendar Jun-11-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Deeb, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 for Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RODRICK FOODS, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution. Request For Judicial Notice.

Defendant Rodrick Foods, Inc.'s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is denied. California favors a policy of disposing of cases on the merits. (CCP 583.130.) Weighing the factors set forth in CRC 3.1342, dismissal is not warranted for plaintiff's delay in formally serving Rodrick. This case involves a simple trip and fall and the incident occurred in December of 2013. Rodrick was first notified of the claim in April 2014 when plaintiff's counsel contacted the claims representative from Rodrick's third party administrator. The claims representative investigated the matter and denied the claim. The attorney who originally handled the claim for plaintiff retired and some delay in prosecution of the action was due to new counsel taking over the case. While there were lapses in communications between the parties, plaintiff mailed a copy of the complaint to a claims supervisor in August 2017, which was within 2 years of the filing of this case. (Deeb Dec., Ex. H.) The interests of justice would not be served by dismissing this action as Rodrick had notice of the claim fairly soon after the incident occurred and any prejudice from the excusable delay in formal service does not outweigh the policy to resolve cases on the merits.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)
